item.text: The repo is very small: one HTML app plus one behavior test file. I’m going to read those closely and treat tests as the clearest record of “binding” behavior where there isn’t separate documentation.

item.text: I found no README or ADR-style document, so the strongest sources are the UI copy, executable app code, and behavior tests. I’m pulling line-numbered evidence now so the answer can point the maintainer to exact locations.

1. CONSTRAINTS AND WHY

- Single-file browser app: PocketLedger is implemented in `index.html` with embedded CSS and JavaScript. This keeps the app deployable/openable as a plain static page with no build step shown in the project.
- Browser-local persistence only: expenses are saved under `localStorage` key `pocketledger.expenses.v1`. The UI says expenses are “stored on this browser,” so the reason is local personal expense tracking without a backend/account system.
- EUR-only money model: the form says “EUR only,” formatting uses `Intl.NumberFormat(..., { currency: "EUR" })`, and CSV exports `Amount EUR`. Amounts are normalized to integer cents to avoid floating-point money drift.
- Valid expense shape is strict: saved entries must have string `id`, positive integer `amountCents`, string `category`, valid `YYYY-MM-DD` date, string `note`, and string `createdAt`. Invalid or malformed storage is ignored so corrupt local data does not break the app.
- Amounts must be greater than zero with up to two decimals: this matches currency precision and prevents zero/negative/non-money entries.
- Category is required; note is optional: the app’s form and normalization enforce category as the organizing dimension while allowing lightweight entry when no note is needed.
- Dates must be valid local calendar keys in `YYYY-MM-DD`: month totals, sorting, filtering, and stored validation all depend on string slicing/comparison of that format.
- The dashboard is current-month oriented: month total and category totals use the active month at page load. This supports the app’s “this month” spending view.
- Expense display is newest first: rows sort by descending date, then descending creation timestamp. The reason is stated in UI copy: “Newest entries first.”
- Category filtering is exact-match against saved categories: categories are derived from existing expenses and sorted alphabetically, so users filter by real ledger categories rather than a separate taxonomy.
- CSV export includes every saved expense, sorted newest first, with RFC-style escaping for commas, quotes, and newlines. This provides portable backup/export without server involvement.
- Export is disabled when there are no expenses: prevents generating an empty file and matches the tested button behavior.
- Delete is immediate by expense id: each row carries `data-delete-id`, and deletion rewrites the saved array. This keeps the local ledger simple and id-based.
- HTML escaping is required for rendered expense fields and dynamic empty-state text. The reason is user-entered category/note content is injected into `innerHTML`.
- Responsive, accessible static UI: the page has semantic labels, `aria-live` regions, screen-reader-only captions/action text, and mobile breakpoints. This keeps the single-page ledger usable on small screens and assistive tech.

2. SOURCES

- `index.html`: app title, local-storage UI copy, form fields, EUR-only hint, category/date/note constraints, CSV button, table structure: lines 501-608.
- `index.html`: storage key, EUR formatter, date/month helpers, amount parsing, date validation, id generation, expense normalization: lines 618-698.
- `index.html`: stored expense validation, malformed-storage fallback, localStorage load/save: lines 700-741.
- `index.html`: month totals, category totals, category list/filtering, EUR formatting, CSV escaping/export, newest-first sorting: lines 743-835.
- `index.html`: category filter rendering, category total rendering, escaped row rendering, HTML escaping: lines 838-902.
- `index.html`: current-month initialization, add/delete/filter/export event handlers, empty-state behavior, exported test API: lines 904-1004.
- `index.html`: layout, responsive behavior, table overflow, screen-reader-only utility, accessible visual structure: lines 1-493.
- `pocketledger.behavior.test.js`: behavior-test harness requires `window.PocketLedger`: lines 11-35.
- `pocketledger.behavior.test.js`: amount normalization to integer cents, trimming category/note: lines 38-51.
- `pocketledger.behavior.test.js`: month-only totals and category totals: lines 53-88.
- `pocketledger.behavior.test.js`: CSV headers and escaping: lines 90-112.
- `pocketledger.behavior.test.js`: malformed storage fallback: lines 113-118.
- `pocketledger.behavior.test.js`: add/delete flow, localStorage persistence, count/month total updates: lines 120-233.
- `pocketledger.behavior.test.js`: category filtering and current-month category totals: lines 235-348.
- `pocketledger.behavior.test.js`: export button downloads all saved expenses as `pocketledger-expenses.csv`: lines 350-505.

3. OPEN QUESTIONS

- There is no README, product brief, ADR, package manifest, or design note explaining original product goals beyond the app and tests.
- The project does not state why EUR was chosen instead of configurable currency.
- The project does not state whether data should remain browser-local forever or eventually sync/import.
- The project does not state browser support targets.
- The project does not document whether current-month totals should update after midnight without reload.
- The project does not state whether delete should require confirmation or support undo.
- The project does not explain whether category suggestions are fixed defaults, user-managed, or only convenience seeds.
